label: Graph RAG & structured retrieval

node.label: Graph RAG & structured retrieval
node.definition: Graph RAG retrieves entities, relations, communities, and multi-hop paths from an explicit graph before grounding generation or synthesis.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Graph RAG & structured retrieval is to connect Retrieval-augmented generation, Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas to downstream capabilities including Neural search, RAG & question answering, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Autonomous research & AI scientist systems.
examples: substrate for Neural search, RAG & question answering
substrate for Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
substrate for Autonomous research & AI scientist systems
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Retrieval-augmented generation, Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.

dependencies.rationale: Graph RAG & structured retrieval stores, selects, or obtains information through Retrieval-augmented generation.
Graph RAG & structured retrieval stores, selects, or obtains information through Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
Graph RAG & structured retrieval uses Message passing & neighborhood aggregation as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
